Just now

--

Python is one of the easiest programming languages to learn. It is simple, readable and widely used for web development, data science, artificial intelligence, automation, game development, and many other fields. Because of its simple syntax, Python is often the first programming language beginners choose to learn. It was created by Guido Van Rossum and was released in 1991.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Why should beginners learn python?

• Easy syntax

• Free and open source

• Huge community

• Works on Windows Linux and Mac

• Used in AI, Machine Learning, Web Development ,Automation ,Data Science and Cyber Security.
Python syntax:-
Syntax means set of rules for writing a code.
print("Hello World")
• Print( ) displays output.

• Python doesn't require semicolons.

• Indentation(empty space added at the beginning of a line of code to create a visual offset) is important.
Basic concepts of python

• Variables: Variables are containers used to store data so your program can remember and use it later.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Example:-
name= "John"
age= 20
marks= 89
• Name stores text.

• Age stores numbers.
2. Data Types: Data types defines the category of a value stored in a variable.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

• Integer: Stores whole numbers.
age= 18
• Float: Stores decimal numbers.
price= 45.99
• String: Use to represent sequence of characters.
name= "Alice"
• Boolean: Stores true or false.
is_student= True3. Taking User Input: Python allows users to enter information.
name= input("Enter your name: ")
print(name)

• input( ) takes input.

• print( ) displays it.
4.Operators: Operators are Special symbols used to perform Calculations and logical manipulations on variables and values called operands.

• Arithmetic Operators: These operators are used to perform mathematical Calculations like addition, subtraction, multiplication and division on values.

• Operator Symbols: +, — , / , * , %
Example:
a=5
b=10
print("addition" ,a+b)
• Comparison Operators: These operators are used to compare two values and evaluate to a single Boolean result either true or false.

• Operator Symbols: ==, > , <, > =, < = , !=
a=10
b=20
print("a==b ;", a==b)
print("a!=b ;", a!=b)
print("a>b ;", a>b)
print("a<b ;", a<b)
print("a>=b ;", a>=b)
print("a<=b ;", a<=b)
• Logical Operators: Logical operators are symbol used to combine or modify multiple conditions.

• Operators : AND, OR, NOT.
a=10
b=20
# Logical AND
print(a>5 and b>15)# Logical OR
print(a>15 or b>15)
#Logical NOT
print(not(a>15))
5. Conditional Statements (if -else): Conditional statements are the decision making statements used in python.
Example:
age=18
if age>=18:
print("You can vote")
else:
print("You cannot vote")This if-else statement checks a condition. If age is 18 or more python prints “You can vote” otherwise it prints “You cannot vote”.
6. Loops: Loops repeat the same task multiple times until a specific condition is met.

• For Loop: Use for loop when the number of repetitions is known.
for i in range(5):
print(i)In the above code, range(5) generates the numbers 0 to 4, and the loop prints each number one by one.

• While Loop: Use while loop when it depends on a condition.
count= 1
while count <= 5:
print(count)
count += 1
And the above code,the loop starts with count = 1 and continues printing the value until count becomes greater than 5.
7. Functions: Functions are reusable block of codes.

• Avoid repeating code

• Makes programmes easier to understand.

• Easy to maintain.
Example:
def greet():
print("Welcome!")
greet()In the above example, the function greet( ) is created using the def keyword. The print (“welcome!”) statement is inside the function. When greet( ) is called python executes the function and displays “welcome!” on the screen.
8. Lists: Lists store multiple values in one variable.
Example:
fruits = ["Apple","Banana","Mango"]Access an item from list:
print(fruits[0])Output: Apple

• Assessing items from a list means retrieving an item from the list using its index number. In python, indexing starts from 0, so the first item has an index of 0.

• Indexing: Indexing is a technique used to assess individual elements within an ordered Sequence.
9. Dictionaries: A dictionary stores data as key - value pairs.

• A key is the name or label used to identify a piece of information.

• A value is the actual information associated with that key.
Example:
student ={
"name" : "John",
"age" : 20
}Access data:
print(student["name"])Output: John
Assessing data in a dictionary means retrieving a value by using its key. In Python, you place the key inside square brackets [ ] to get the corresponding value.
10. Tuple: A Tuple is a collection of items that stores multiple values in a single variable.

• Unlike list, tuple cannot be changed after it is created.

• Tuples are written using parenthesis( ).
Example:
fruits = ("Apple","Banana", "Mango")
print(fruits)Output: (‘ Apple ’ , ’ Banana ’ , ‘ Mango ’)
Accessing Data in a Tuple:
fruits = ("Apple","Banana", "Mango")
print(fruits[1])Output: Banana

• You can assess items in a tuple using their index number. like lists tuple indexing start from 0.
11. Set: A set is a collection of unique items. It does not allow duplicate values, and the items are unordered, which means they do not have a fixed position. Sets are written using curly braces{ }.
Example:
numbers = {1, 2, 3, 2, 1}
print(numbers)Output: {1 , 2 , 3}

• The Order of items in a set may be different each time you run the program because sets are unordered.

• A set does not support indexing, So you cannot assess items using an index number like sets or tuples. However, you can use a for loop to access each item one by one.
12. Comments: Comments explain code. Comments are ignored by python but help humans to understand the code. A comment can be single-line or multi - line comment.

• To write a single and comment in python, add a hash(#) symbol at the beginning of the comment.

• To write a multi-line comment in python enclose the comment inside triple quotes( ’’’ ’’’ or ””” ”””)
Example:
# This is a Comment
print("Hello")
Conclusion:
Python is an excellent programming language for beginners because it is simple, powerful and widely used in many industries. By understanding concepts such as variables ,data types, operators .conditions, loops, functions, lists, dictionaries, tuples , sets and comments you will have a strong foundation for learning more advanced python topics. Keep practising regularly write small programs, and experiments with your own ideas. The more you practise, the more confident you will become in python programming.
